content.verdict: Perivolas is the original Santorini icon — the most photographed infinity pool in the world is here, where the water appears to spill directly into the caldera 300 meters below. With just 20 cave suites and villas built from restored 300-year-old wine-cave dwellings, Perivolas is less a hotel than a private cliff-side hamlet. The design is pure Cycladic minimalism: raw white lime-washed walls, vaulted cave ceilings, hand-smoothed furnishings, no televisions to distract from the view. Adults-only, intensely private, and widely considered one of the most romantic hotels on earth. This is where you come if you want the best of Santorini with zero compromise.
content.best_room: The Perivolas Suite with private heated pool — a fully restored cave dwelling with a large private plunge pool on the terrace, direct caldera view, and complete privacy. For the ultimate splurge, the Private Villa has its own garden, multi-room layout, and a private heated pool with uninterrupted sunset view.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Private car from JTR, welcome by your personal host, sunset champagne on your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Swim in the iconic infinity pool at dawn before other guests wake, breakfast in suite, afternoon wine tasting at nearby Domaine Sigalas.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Private yacht day around the caldera with lunch on board and swim at Red Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning couples' spa treatment in the cave-carved wellness center, lazy afternoon at the pool, dinner at Perivolas' own cliff-edge restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Explore Oia on foot before the cruise crowds arrive (7-9am is magic), lunch at M
itinerary_7_nights.description: Explore Oia on foot before the cruise crowds arrive (7-9am is magic), lunch at Melitini, siesta, sunset from your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to a traditional Santorini winery and the Akrotiri ruins
itinerary_7_nights.description: Day trip to a traditional Santorini winery and the Akrotiri ruins.

caveats: Perivolas is very expensive, especially in peak summer — expect $1,500-$4,000 per night in July/August.
Many steps throughout the property; not ideal for guests with limited mobility.
Like all Oia hotels, there's no beach — plan a day trip to Perissa or Kamari if you want dark-sand swimming.
Book at least 6-9 months ahead for summer; the 20-suite capacity fills fast.
Some suites are set slightly back from the edge — pay for a front-row unit or it's not worth the price.

faqs.a: Yes — Perivolas enforces a strict 18+ policy, which is part of why it's so revered among honeymooners.

faqs.q: Is the famous infinity pool open to all guests?
faqs.a: Yes. The main infinity pool is available to all resort guests and is rarely crowded due to the small number of suites.

faqs.q: How many suites does Perivolas have?
faqs.a: Only 20 — one of the smallest luxury properties in Santorini, which is why it feels so intimate.
6-9 months ahead for summer stays, 3-4 months for shoulder season.

faqs.q: Is dinner on site good enough, or should we go out?
faqs.a: The in-house restaurant is genuinely excellent — a destination in its own right. But do walk into Oia village at least twice (Ambrosia and Melitini are worth it).
Smart casual — resort wear during the day, smart for dinner. No shorts at dinner.
